papers.title: Vidu S1: A Real-Time Interactive Video Generation Model
papers.abstract: Abstract Vidu S1 is a real-time interactive video generation model that supports voice-controlled digital character animation with infinite-length output and high frame rate on consumer hardware.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct We introduce Vidu S1, a real-time interactive video generation model supporting voice control of digital characters. Users can control video generation content at any moment through voice instructions. Vidu S1 supports infinite-length real-time video generation without blurring, drift, or visual distortion. Built with TurboDiffusion and TurboServe, Vidu S1 outputs 540p real-time videos at up to 42 FPS on regular consumer GPUs. Users can upload custom images of real people, anime, and pets, and choose different voice tones for personalized experiences. Experiments show that Vidu S1 achieves the best performance across all test metrics while fully meeting real-time inference requirements. A playable online demo is available at https://vidu.com/vidu-stream.

ru.title: Голосовой контроль видеогенерации в реальном времени на обычных GPU
ru.desc: Vidu S1 — это модель генерации видео в реальном времени, которая позволяет управлять анимацией цифровых персонажей голосовыми командами. Модель использует диффузионные архитектуры (TurboDiffusion) и оптимизированные механизмы обслуживания (TurboServe) для достижения высокой производительности на потребительском оборудовании. Система способна генерировать видео бесконечной длины с разрешением 540p на частоте до 42 FPS без артефактов размытия и визуальных искажений. Пользователи могут загружать собственные изображения и управлять видеогенерацией через голосовые инструкции с возможностью выбора различных тембров голоса.

en.title: Voice-Controlled Video Generation at Your Fingertips!
en.desc: Vidu S1 is an advanced model for generating interactive videos in real-time, allowing users to animate digital characters using voice commands. It can produce videos of any length without losing quality, ensuring smooth visuals without blurring or distortion. The model operates efficiently on standard consumer hardware, delivering high frame rates of up to 42 FPS at 540p resolution. Users can personalize their experience by uploading images and selecting different voice tones, making the interaction more engaging and tailored to individual preferences.

zh.title: 实时语音控制，创造无限视频
zh.desc: Vidu S1 是一个实时互动视频生成模型，支持通过语音控制数字角色的动画。用户可以随时通过语音指令控制视频生成的内容。该模型能够生成无限长度的实时视频，且不会出现模糊、漂移或视觉失真。Vidu S1 在普通消费级 GPU 上以高达 42 帧每秒的速度输出 540p 的实时视频，提供个性化的体验。

papers.title: Video-Oasis: Rethinking Evaluation of Video Understanding
papers.abstract: Abstract Video-Oasis diagnostics reveal that half of existing video benchmarks can be solved without visual input, exposing significant capability gaps in current video understanding models.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct The inherent complexity of video understanding makes it difficult to determine whether Video-LLM benchmark performance stems from visual perception, linguistic reasoning, or knowledge priors. While many benchmarks have emerged to assess high-level reasoning, shared criteria for evaluating video understanding remain largely overlooked. Instead of introducing yet another benchmark, we take a step back to re-examine the criteria for evaluating video understanding. In this work, we introduce Video-Oasis, a sustainable diagnostic suite for systematically auditing existing video understanding benchmarks. This audit reveals that 55\% of existing benchmark samples are solvable without visual input or temporal context. After filtering these shortcuts, the remaining video-native challenges expose a substantial capability gap: state-of-the-art models perform only marginally above random guessing. Building on these findings, we use the distilled challenges as a testbed to investigate which algorithmic design choices contribute to robust video understanding. We hope our work provides a practical foundation for constructing rigorous video benchmarks and evaluating future Video-LLMs. Code is available at https://github.com/sejong-rcv/Video-Oasis.

ru.title: Диагностика слепых пятен в бенчмарках видеопонимания
ru.desc: Исследование показывает, что более половины существующих бенчмарков для оценки видеопонимания можно решить без использования визуальной информации, что свидетельствует о серьёзных пробелах в возможностях современных Video-LLM моделей. Авторы предлагают Video-Oasis — диагностический инструмент для систематической проверки качества бенчмарков видеопонимания. Анализ выявил, что 55% образцов в существующих бенчмарках решаются без визуального контекста или временной информации, обнажив значительный разрыв между производительностью моделей и случайным угадыванием. Исследование предоставляет практическую основу для создания более строгих бенчмарков и разработки более надёжных алгоритмов видеопонимания.

en.title: Bridging the Gap in Video Understanding Models
en.desc: This paper introduces Video-Oasis, a diagnostic tool designed to evaluate existing video understanding benchmarks. The authors found that many benchmarks can be solved without using visual information, indicating a significant gap in the capabilities of current video understanding models. By filtering out these non-visual shortcuts, they reveal that state-of-the-art models struggle to perform better than random guessing on genuine video challenges. The study aims to establish better criteria for assessing video understanding and to guide the development of more effective video learning models.

zh.title: 重新审视视频理解基准的必要性
zh.desc: 这篇论文揭示了现有视频基准测试中有一半可以在没有视觉输入的情况下解决，显示了当前视频理解模型的能力差距。视频理解的复杂性使得很难判断基准测试的表现是来自视觉感知、语言推理还是知识先验。我们提出了Video-Oasis，这是一个可持续的诊断工具，用于系统审计现有的视频理解基准。通过审计，我们发现55%的基准样本可以在没有视觉输入或时间上下文的情况下解决，剩余的挑战显示出当前模型的能力仍然有限。

papers.title: Why Can't I Open My Drawer? Mitigating Object-Driven Shortcuts in Zero-Shot Compositional Action Recognition
papers.abstract: Abstract RCORE addresses object-driven shortcuts in zero-shot compositional action recognition by using co-occurrence prior regularization and temporal order regularization to improve compositional generalization.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Zero-Shot Compositional Action Recognition (ZS-CAR) requires recognizing novel verb-object combinations composed of previously observed primitives. In this work, we tackle a key failure mode: models predict verbs via object-driven shortcuts (i.e., relying on the labeled object class) rather than temporal evidence. We argue that sparse compositional supervision and verb-object learning asymmetry can promote object-driven shortcut learning. Our analysis with proposed diagnostic metrics shows that existing methods overfit to training co-occurrence patterns and underuse temporal verb cues, resulting in weak generalization to unseen compositions. To address object-driven shortcuts, we propose Robust COmpositional REpresentations (RCORE) with two components. Co-occurrence Prior Regularization (CPR) adds explicit supervision for unseen compositions and regularizes the model against frequent co-occurrence priors by treating them as hard negatives. Temporal Order Regularization for Composition (TORC) enforces temporal-order sensitivity to learn temporally grounded verb representations. Across Sth-com and EK100-com, RCORE reduces shortcut diagnostics and consequently improves compositional generalization.

ru.title: Избегаем объектных ловушек в распознавании составных действий через темпоральную регуляризацию
ru.desc: В работе решается проблема распознавания композиционных действий в нулевой обучающей выборке, где модели ошибочно полагаются на признаки объектов вместо темпоральной информации о глаголах. Авторы предлагают RCORE — метод с двумя компонентами: регуляризация на основе co-occurrence prior (CPR) добавляет явный контроль для невиденных комбинаций и обучает модель игнорировать частые совместные появления объектов и глаголов; регуляризация временного порядка (TORC) принуждает модель к обучению представлений глаголов, чувствительных к временной последовательности действий. Диагностические метрики показывают, что существующие методы переобучаются на паттернах co-occurrence в данных обучения и недостаточно используют темпоральные подсказки о глаголах. Экспериментальные результаты на датасетах Sth-com и EK100-com демонстрируют, что RCORE существенно улучшает композиционную обобщающую способность модели.

en.title: Enhancing Action Recognition by Tackling Object-Driven Shortcuts
en.desc: The paper introduces RCORE, a method designed to enhance zero-shot compositional action recognition by addressing the issue of object-driven shortcuts. These shortcuts occur when models rely on object labels instead of temporal evidence to predict actions. RCORE employs two key strategies: Co-occurrence Prior Regularization (CPR) to mitigate reliance on frequent co-occurrence patterns, and Temporal Order Regularization for Composition (TORC) to ensure that the model learns to recognize actions based on their temporal context. The results demonstrate that RCORE significantly improves the model's ability to generalize to new verb-object combinations by reducing shortcut learning.

zh.title: 提升组合泛化能力的RCORE方法
zh.desc: RCORE是一种针对零-shot组合动作识别中物体驱动快捷方式的问题的解决方案。它通过引入共现先验正则化和时间顺序正则化来提高组合泛化能力。研究表明，现有方法容易过拟合训练数据中的共现模式，而忽视时间线索，导致对未见组合的泛化能力较弱。RCORE通过显式监督和时间敏感性学习，减少了物体驱动的快捷方式，从而提升了模型的表现。

papers.title: UniClawBench: A Universal Benchmark for Proactive Agents on Real-World Tasks
papers.abstract: Abstract UniClawBench introduces a capability-driven benchmark for evaluating proactive agents in real-world environments using live Docker container evaluation and closed-loop assessment with multiple agent roles.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct The rapid development of large language models and multimodal large language models has accelerated the emergence of proactive agents capable of operating everyday tools and assisting users in real-world environments. However, existing benchmarks struggle to evaluate such agents effectively, as they often rely on sandboxed environments and single-turn evaluation paradigms. Moreover, their scenario-based task taxonomies mix multiple model capabilities within the same task category, making it difficult to identify the root causes of agent failures. To address these limitations, we introduce UniClawBench, the first capability-driven benchmark designed to evaluate proactive agents in dynamic, real-world settings. UniClawBench is built around five foundational model capabilities: Skill Usage, Exploration, Long-Context Reasoning, Multimodal Understanding, and Cross-Platform Coordination. Based on these capabilities, we design 400 bilingual real-world tasks. Unlike previous benchmarks that rely on static, pre-recorded answers, our benchmark evaluates agents in live Docker containers using fine-grained, step-by-step completion checkpoints. Furthermore, we design a closed-loop evaluation strategy comprising an executor agent, a hidden supervisor agent, and a user agent to simulate realistic multi-turn human feedback without leaking grading criteria. To disentangle base model capabilities from framework-level design choices, we evaluate state-of-the-art models under multiple agent frameworks. Through comprehensive comparisons across both models and frameworks, we show how base model capabilities and agent framework designs jointly shape performance in real-world environments. To facilitate future research, we make our benchmark and code publicly available at https://github.com/HKU-MMLab/UniClawBench.

ru.title: Способности, а не сценарии: оценка агентов в реальных условиях
ru.desc: UniClawBench представляет собой первый бенчмарк на основе способностей модели для оценки проактивных агентов в динамичных реальных окружениях. Бенчмарк содержит 400 двуязычных задач, сосредоточенных на пяти фундаментальных способностях: использование инструментов, исследование, рассуждение на длинных контекстах, мультимодальное понимание и координация между платформами. В отличие от существующих подходов, UniClawBench использует живые Docker-контейнеры и пошаговую оценку завершения задач вместо статических предзаписанных ответов. Авторы разработали стратегию замкнутого цикла оценки с агентом-исполнителем, скрытым супервизором и агентом-пользователем, чтобы симулировать реалистичную многооборотную обратную связь без утечки критериев оценки.

en.title: Evaluating Proactive Agents in Real-World Scenarios
en.desc: UniClawBench is a new benchmark designed to evaluate proactive agents in real-world environments, focusing on their capabilities rather than just their performance in isolated tasks. It introduces a closed-loop evaluation system that simulates realistic interactions, allowing for a more accurate assessment of agent behavior. The benchmark is built around five key capabilities: Skill Usage, Exploration, Long-Context Reasoning, Multimodal Understanding, and Cross-Platform Coordination, with 400 bilingual tasks to test these skills. By using live Docker containers for evaluation, UniClawBench provides a dynamic testing environment that helps identify the strengths and weaknesses of different agent frameworks and models.

zh.title: 能力驱动的主动代理评估基准
zh.desc: UniClawBench 是一个能力驱动的基准测试，用于评估在真实环境中运行的主动代理。它通过使用实时 Docker 容器评估和闭环评估，解决了现有基准测试在动态环境中评估代理的不足。该基准围绕五个基础模型能力设计了 400 个双语真实任务，能够更好地识别代理失败的根本原因。我们还设计了一个闭环评估策略，模拟真实的多轮人类反馈，以提高评估的准确性和有效性。

papers.title: Ideas Have Genomes: Benchmarking Scientific Lineage Reasoning and Lineage-Grounded Idea Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract A benchmark for scientific lineage reasoning and idea generation is introduced, organizing scientific works as genetic-like Idea Genome objects and evaluating both reasoning and generation capabilities.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Scientific ideas rarely start from a blank page. They inherit mechanisms, repair known limitations, and recombine pieces of earlier work, much like biological genomes. Current benchmarks still say little about whether AI systems can follow this inheritance structure. We present IdeaGene-Bench (IG-Bench), a benchmark for scientific lineage reasoning and lineage-grounded idea generation. IG-Bench is organized around the IdeaGene framework: each paper or proposal is represented as a set of minimal, typed, evidence-grounded Idea Genome objects, and a GenomeDiff aligns these objects to record inheritance, mutation, loss, external import, and novel insertion under six operational evolutionary dynamics. The benchmark contains 1,961 golden lineage traces, 1,085 curated Idea Genome objects, and 920 pairwise GenomeDiff records across 10 scientific domains. It supports two evaluations. IG-Exam (42 task types, 1,029 instances) tests closed-form lineage reasoning across Idea Genome abstraction, inheritance tracing, evolutionary reasoning, and lineage verification. IG-Arena evaluates generation with a lineage-conditioned Population-Evolution Score(PES), asking whether a proposal can be inserted as a coherent descendant of a given lineage population: it should inherit the right Idea Genome objects, vary meaningfully from nearby work, and offer selection value for future research. Experiments on 14 LLM-based scientists expose a compositional bottleneck. The strongest system reaches only 27.3% exact accuracy on lineage reasoning, and structured lineage context reshuffles system rankings rather than helping every participant uniformly.
affiliations: University of Chinese Academy of Sciences
University of Science and Technology of China

ru.title: Научные идеи как геномы: бенчмарк для оценки понимания эволюции идей в LLM
ru.desc: В работе представлен бенчмарк IdeaGene-Bench для оценки способности больших языковых моделей понимать и генерировать научные идеи, следуя их эволюционной линии наследования. Научные работы организованы в виде объектов Idea Genome, которые отслеживают наследование механизмов, мутации и рекомбинации идей из предыдущих исследований, подобно биологическим геномам. Бенчмарк содержит две задачи: IG-Exam для проверки рассуждений о линейности идей и IG-Arena для оценки генерации новых предложений, которые логически встраиваются в существующую популяцию научных работ. Эксперименты показали, что даже сильнейшие LLM достигают только 27,3% точности в понимании линейности наследования научных идей.

en.title: Unlocking Scientific Creativity: The Idea Genome Approach
en.desc: This paper introduces a new benchmark called IdeaGene-Bench (IG-Bench) for evaluating how well AI systems can reason about scientific ideas and generate new ones based on existing knowledge. It organizes scientific works into genetic-like structures called Idea Genome objects, which capture the inheritance and evolution of ideas. The benchmark includes a variety of tasks to assess both the reasoning capabilities and the generation of ideas, focusing on how well AI can trace lineage and create coherent new proposals. The findings reveal that current AI models struggle with these tasks, highlighting a significant challenge in their ability to understand and build upon existing scientific knowledge.

zh.title: 科学创意的谱系推理与生成基准
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种用于科学谱系推理和创意生成的基准，称为IdeaGene-Bench（IG-Bench）。该基准将科学作品组织为类似基因组的Idea Genome对象，并评估推理和生成能力。IG-Bench包含1961个黄金谱系痕迹和1085个经过整理的Idea Genome对象，支持多种评估任务。实验表明，当前的AI系统在谱系推理方面存在组合瓶颈，最强的系统在推理准确性上仅达到27.3%。

papers.title: LongE2V: Long-Horizon Event-based Video Reconstruction, Prediction, and Frame Interpolation with Video Diffusion Models
papers.abstract: Abstract LongE2V enables high-quality video recovery from sparse event streams by leveraging pre-trained video diffusion priors and addressing temporal stability and frame interpolation challenges.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Recovering high-quality video from sparse event streams is a challenging task. Regression methods often blur textures, while existing generative models struggle with long-term stability. We propose LongE2V, a novel approach that leverages pre-trained video diffusion priors to jointly handle event-based video reconstruction, prediction, and frame interpolation. By fine-tuning a foundational video model, our approach achieves high data efficiency and superior perceptual quality. We introduce Autoregressive Unrolling and Adaptive Context Switching to mitigate temporal drift in extremely long sequences. We also propose Reencoding Alignment with Cross Residual Correction to ensure precise bidirectional consistency during frame interpolation. Furthermore, Event Voxel Density Augmentation ensures robustness across varying sensor resolutions. Extensive experiments on real-world benchmarks demonstrate that LongE2V outperforms state-of-the-art methods across all three tasks, exhibiting exceptional temporal coherence and zero-shot generalization. Project page: https://cdfan0627.github.io/LongE2V-page/
affiliations: National Yang Ming Chiao Tung University, Taiwan

ru.title: Восстановление видео из событийных потоков с помощью диффузионных приоров
ru.desc: LongE2V — это метод восстановления высококачественного видео из разреженных потоков событий с использованием предварительно обученных диффузионных моделей видео. Авторы решают проблему временной нестабильности в длинных последовательностях через авторегрессивное развёртывание и адаптивное переключение контекста. Для интерполяции кадров применяется переэнкодирование с коррекцией кросс-остатков, обеспечивающее точную двусторонню согласованность. Метод демонстрирует превосходные результаты на реальных тестовых наборах данных с исключительной временной согласованностью и нулевой обучаемостью.

en.title: Revolutionizing Video Recovery with LongE2V
en.desc: LongE2V is a novel method designed to recover high-quality videos from sparse event streams by utilizing pre-trained video diffusion models. It addresses key challenges such as temporal stability and frame interpolation, which are often problematic in traditional regression and generative models. The approach incorporates techniques like Autoregressive Unrolling and Adaptive Context Switching to reduce temporal drift in long video sequences. Extensive testing shows that LongE2V significantly outperforms existing methods, providing better visual quality and consistency across various tasks.

zh.title: 从稀疏事件流中恢复高质量视频的创新方法
zh.desc: LongE2V是一种新颖的方法，旨在从稀疏事件流中恢复高质量视频。它利用预训练的视频扩散先验，解决了时间稳定性和帧插值的挑战。通过微调基础视频模型，LongE2V实现了高数据效率和卓越的感知质量。该方法在真实世界基准测试中表现优异，超越了现有的最先进技术。

papers.title: Enhancing In-context Panoramic Generation via Geometric-aware Pretraining
papers.abstract: Abstract Canvas360 is a two-stage framework for in-context panoramic generation that combines geometry-aware pretraining with fine-tuning, featuring a large-scale dataset and novel modeling techniques for improved geometric consistency and global coherence.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct In this work, we present Canvas360, a two-stage framework for in-context panoramic generation that combines geometry-aware pretraining with downstream task-specific fine-tuning. To address the lack of large-scale, high-quality training data tailored to in-context panoramic tasks, we propose Canvas360Dataset, a collection of 1M high-quality paired panoramic samples for style transfer, inpainting, outpainting, and editing, enabling effective supervision across diverse in-context generation scenarios. On the modeling side, Canvas360 enhances text-to-panorama generation through parallel depth generation, velocity circular padding, and similarity loss regularization, enabling the model to learn geometry-aware representations, capture object distortion details, and improve geometric consistency and global coherence. Furthermore, empowered by strong panoramic priors, Canvas360 enables a unified in-context panoramic generation framework that supports diverse downstream tasks via token-level concatenation, surpassing prior methods in both task coverage and modeling flexibility. Extensive experiments show that Canvas360 improves panoramic image fidelity, achieving particularly strong performance on the panorama-specific FAED metric and competitive or leading results across the reported quantitative evaluations. More information can be found on our project page: https://zry000.github.io/Canvas360/

ru.title: Единая архитектура для согласованной генерации полнокруглых панорам
ru.desc: Canvas360 — это двухэтапная архитектура для генерации панорамных изображений в контексте, которая объединяет предварительное обучение, учитывающее геометрию сцены, с последующей тонкой настройкой. Авторы создали Canvas360Dataset — большой датасет из 1 млн высококачественных панорамных пар для задач трансформации стиля, инпейнтинга и редактирования. Модель использует параллельную генерацию карт глубины, циклическую паддинг-операцию и регуляризацию через функцию потерь сходства для изучения геометрически согласованных представлений. Предложенный подход поддерживает единую архитектуру для различных задач манипуляции панорамами и демонстрирует превосходные результаты по метрикам качества.

en.title: Revolutionizing Panoramic Image Generation with Canvas360
en.desc: Canvas360 is a two-stage framework designed for generating panoramic images in context, utilizing geometry-aware pretraining followed by task-specific fine-tuning. It introduces a large dataset, Canvas360Dataset, containing 1 million high-quality panoramic samples to enhance training for various tasks like style transfer and inpainting. The framework employs advanced modeling techniques such as parallel depth generation and similarity loss regularization to improve the geometric consistency and coherence of the generated images. Overall, Canvas360 demonstrates superior performance in generating high-fidelity panoramic images compared to previous methods, making it a versatile tool for diverse applications.

zh.title: Canvas360：全景生成的新框架
zh.desc: Canvas360是一个两阶段的框架，用于上下文全景生成，结合了几何感知的预训练和特定任务的微调。为了应对缺乏大规模高质量训练数据的问题，我们提出了Canvas360Dataset，包含100万个高质量的全景样本，支持风格迁移、修补、扩展和编辑等任务。该框架通过并行深度生成、速度循环填充和相似性损失正则化，提升了文本到全景的生成能力，增强了几何一致性和全局连贯性。实验结果表明，Canvas360在全景图像的保真度上表现优异，尤其在全景特定的FAED指标上取得了强劲的表现。

papers.title: DrugGen 2: A disease-aware language model for enhancing drug discovery
papers.abstract: Abstract DrugGen-2 generates small molecules conditioned on disease ontology and target protein sequences through fine-tuning GPT-2 with supervised learning and reinforcement learning using GRPO, achieving superior molecular diversity and binding affinity compared to baseline models.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Current computational approaches for drug design typically focus on generating molecules conditioned on specific targets or general molecular properties, often neglecting the influence of disease context on target behavior and therapeutic outcomes. To address this gap, we introduce DrugGen-2, a novel generative model that designs small molecules conditioned on both disease ontology and target protein sequences. DrugGen-2 was developed by fine-tuning a pre-trained GPT-2 model on a curated dataset of approved drugs linked to their diseases and targets, using a two-step strategy of supervised fine-tuning followed by reinforcement learning via group relative policy optimization (GRPO). This process was guided by reward functions optimizing for chemical validity, novelty, diversity, and high predicted binding affinity. When evaluated on five protein targets relevant to diabetic nephropathy, DrugGen-2 significantly outperformed baseline models (DrugGPT and DrugGen). It demonstrated a superior capacity to generate unique molecules, exhibited greater structural similarity to approved drugs, and achieved improved predicted binding affinities across all targets. Molecular docking analyses further supported these findings, identifying candidate ligands with strong binding potential, including compounds with predicted affinities (-9.917, -9.485, and -9.367) exceeding those of reference drugs such as enalapril for angiotensin-converting enzyme (-8.283). By integrating disease-specific context into molecular generation, DrugGen-2 advances AI-assisted drug discovery, offering a powerful tool for de novo design and drug repurposing that accounts for the complex interplay between diseases and molecular targets.
